sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To view backup snapshots, you must have at least [Project Backup Creator](<Project Backup Creator>) or [Project Read Only](<Project Read Only>) access to the project.
Users with  [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves as
a [Project Read Only](<Project Read Only>) access to the project before they can
view backup policies.

To take or delete a backup snapshot, you must have [Project Backup Creator](<Project Backup Creator>), [Project Backup Manager](<Project Backup Manager>) or
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project. Users with
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves as a
[Project Backup Creator](<Project Backup Creator>), [Project Backup Manager](<Project Backup Manager>),
or [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project before they can take
or delete a backup snapshot.

sections.heading: Delete a Backup Snapshot
sections.body:
> **Note:** If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), you
> can't delete a backup snapshot.You can delete a snapshot using the Atlas CLI, the Atlas Administration API, or the
Atlas UI. To delete all copies of a snapshot affected by a
multi-region [snapshot copy distribution](snapshot-distribution)
policy without deleting the policy itself, delete the snapshot from its
original region.**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo delete the backup snapshot you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas backups snapshots delete <snapshotId> [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas backups snapshots delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-snapshots-delete).**Api:**Atlas Administration API* For non-sharded clusters, delete the snapshot programmatically
  with [Remove One Replica Set Cloud Backup](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2).

* For sharded clusters, delete the snapshot programmatically with
  [Remove One Sharded Cluster Cloud Backup](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2).**Ui:**Atlas UITo delete a snapshot from the Atlas UI, perform the following procedure:

sections.heading: Step 1: Select the snapshot to delete.
sections.body:
Select the snapshot you wish to delete from the list under
the **Snapshots** tab and click trash-alt.

Deleting the original snapshot by any of these methods will propagate
the deletion to all copies of that snapshot.

sections.heading: View Backup Snapshots
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo list cloud backup snapshots for your project and cluster using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas backups snapshots list <clusterName> [options]

```To return the details for the snapshot you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas backups snapshots describe <snapshotId> [options]

```To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas backups snapshot list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-snapshots-list) and
[atlas backups snapshots describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-snapshots-describe).Related Links* [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

* [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)**Ui:**Atlas UIAtlas displays existing snapshots for a given cluster on the
Snapshots page. To view snapshots that Atlas has
already taken:Alternatively, to view a list of all `M10+` clusters with
cloud backup enabled:If you choose to retain backups from a cluster you
terminate, they remain accessible here under their source
cluster name until they expire or you delete them.By default, Atlas displays both on-demand and policy-based
snapshots. To view only policy-based snapshots:* Click **Policy** under **View Snapshots by**.

  Alternatively, click **On-demand** to display only snapshots
  taken by clicking **Take Snapshot Now**.Snapshots taken according to your backup policy display the frequency of
the policy item that generated the snapshot in the
**Frequency** column: `Yearly`, `Monthly`, `Weekly`,
`Daily`, or `Hourly`.> **Note:** 
>
> If overlapping policy items generate the same snapshot, Atlas
> associates the snapshot with the policy item with the longest retention
> time.
>
> For example, if the policy specifies a daily snapshot with two-day
> retention and a weekly snapshot every Saturday with three-week
> retention, then Atlas displays a frequency of Weekly in
> the Frequency column on the Snapshots page for
> the snapshot taken on Saturday and retains the snapshot for three weeks.
> This is because the weekly policy item has a longer retention time than
> the daily policy item.You can change a zone's name in the Atlas UI. If you rename a zone,
old snapshots' tooltip zone names aren't renamed.

sections.heading: Take On-Demand Snapshots
sections.body:
Atlas takes on-demand snapshots immediately, unlike scheduled
snapshots which occur at
[regular intervals](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getgroupclusterbackupschedule). If there is already an on-demand snapshot with
a status of `queued` or `inProgress`, you must wait until Atlas
has completed the on-demand snapshot before taking another. If there is
already a scheduled snapshot with a status of `queued` or
`inProgress`, you may queue an on-demand snapshot.> **Note:** If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy) with
> a specified retention time for on-demand snapshots, you can't
> decrease the retention time for an on-demand snapshot after
> it's taken.**Atlascli:**Atlas CLIYou must have the [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) or
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) role to successfully call these
endpoints.To create a backup snapshot for your project and cluster using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas backups snapshots create <clusterName> [options]

```To watch for a specific backup snapshot to become available using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas backups snapshots watch <snapshotId> [options]

```To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas backups snapshots create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-snapshots-create) and
[atlas backups snapshots watch](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-snapshots-watch).Related Links* [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

* [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)**Api:**Atlas Administration APITo take on-demand snapshots using the API, see
[Take One On-Demand Snapshot](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-takegroupclusterbackupsnapshots).**Ui:**Atlas UITo take an on-demand snapshot:- Click the  button below the cluster name.

- Click Take Snapshot Now.* In the On-Demand Snapshot modal, enter the
  following information:

  - In the Retention box, enter the number of days
    that you want Atlas to retain the snapshot.

  - In the Description box, enter a descriptive name
    for the snapshot.

* Click Take Snapshot.To view an on-demand snapshot:Click Snapshots for the cluster.> **Note:** The Take Snapshot Now button also appears on the
> Snapshots page for the cluster.
